conflicts.title: README test count outdated: claims '98 passed', actual suite is 256 tests
conflicts.description: README.md line 176 states 'most recent result: 98 passed'. The infrastructure-profile.json (test_count_approx field) and the full test suite catalog document 256 tests across 48 test files. The README has not been updated after significant test suite expansion.
conflicts.resolution_hint: Update README.md test badge line to '256 passed' after next full suite run.

conflicts.title: PLAN.md Phase 1 scope says 'adapter interface stubs only' but concrete subprocess adapters are fully implemented
conflicts.description: PLAN.md Planned Deliverables Phase 1 lists 'adapter interface definitions' as a Phase 1 deliverable and relegates 'executor adapters for claude_code and codex' to Phase 2. In practice, ClaudeCodeAdapter, CodexAdapter, PythonAdapter, ClineAdapter, and OpenCodeAdapter are all fully implemented in src/devforge/executors/adapters.py with SubprocessTransport. The project has advanced beyond Phase 1 scope without updating PLAN.md to reflect Phase 2+ completion.
conflicts.doc_reference: PLAN.md:978-988 — Phase 1 deliverables list; PLAN.md:990-993 — Phase 2 includes 'executor adapters for claude_code and codex'
conflicts.code_reference: src/devforge/executors/adapters.py — ClaudeCodeAdapter, CodexAdapter, PythonAdapter, ClineAdapter, OpenCodeAdapter all implemented; src/devforge/executors/subprocess_transport.py — SubprocessTransport fully implemented
conflicts.resolution_hint: Update PLAN.md Phase 1/2 deliverable sections to reflect current implementation state. Consider adding a 'Current Status' section.

conflicts.title: PLAN.md Phase 2 includes 'persistence layer and project scheduler' but both are fully implemented
conflicts.description: PLAN.md Phase 2 lists 'persistence layer' and 'project scheduler' as future deliverables. Both are fully implemented: WorkspacePersistence with SQLiteSnapshotStore/EventStore/FileArtifactStore/JsonMemoryStore exists in src/devforge/persistence/, and select_workset() is implemented in src/devforge/scheduler/. This creates a misleading picture of project maturity for new contributors.
conflicts.resolution_hint: Mark Phase 2 items as 'completed' in PLAN.md or restructure into 'What is built' vs 'What is planned'.

conflicts.title: executors/stubs.py is now a pure re-export of adapters.py, not a stub implementation
conflicts.description: src/devforge/executors/stubs.py contains only backward-compatible re-exports from adapters.py (ClaudeCodeAdapter, CodexAdapter, PythonAdapter, ClineAdapter, OpenCodeAdapter). The file name 'stubs' implies test-only stub implementations, but the actual test stubs are inline in tests using MockLLMClient + fixture snapshots. This creates naming confusion: 'stubs.py' exports production adapters, not stubs. The file-catalog.json describes it as 'Stub adapter implementations used in test mode to simulate executor responses' — this description is inaccurate.
conflicts.doc_reference: .allforai/bootstrap/file-catalog.json — M002.key_files[10].intent for stubs.py: 'Stub adapter implementations used in test mode to simulate executor responses without calling real CLI tools'
conflicts.code_reference: src/devforge/executors/stubs.py:1-11 — file body is only re-export aliases to adapters.py, no stub logic
conflicts.resolution_hint: Either rename stubs.py to compat.py (backward-compat re-exports) or add actual stub implementations for test isolation. Update file-catalog.json description.

conflicts.title: PLAN.md role list includes 'interaction_designer' and 'ui_designer' but PLAN.md role system section omits them
conflicts.description: PLAN.md Role System section (line 347) lists 8 initial built-in roles including 'interaction_designer' and 'ui_designer'. The ROLE_REGISTRY in src/devforge/roles/registry.py correctly includes both. However, the PLAN.md executor system section and phase→role→executor examples only reference the 6 operational roles (product_manager, execution_planner, technical_architect, software_engineer, qa_engineer, integration_owner), creating an incomplete picture for contributors.
conflicts.doc_reference: PLAN.md:347-360 — role list includes interaction_designer and ui_designer; PLAN.md:381-394 — executor section examples omit design roles
conflicts.code_reference: src/devforge/roles/registry.py:33-47 — interaction_designer and ui_designer are present with allowed_phases: analysis_design
conflicts.resolution_hint: Minor inconsistency only. PLAN.md executor section examples could mention that design roles map to claude_code/opencode in analysis_design phase.
